summarization_0: The green card application journey for you and Kayla begins with determining eligibility and gathering essential documentation such as proof of marriage, financial records, and identification. The process involves filing Form I-130 by Kayla, followed by your submission of Form I-485, along with attending a medical examination conducted by a USCIS-designated civil surgeon. Financial stability is critical, requiring detailed income documentation and possibly an Affidavit of Support, with consideration of your combined financial situation and potential legal advice. Preparation for the USCIS interview includes organizing joint financial documents, practicing responses, and attending mock interviews with your attorney, Maria Lopez, who also provides real-time support during the interview. Post-interview, you must monitor your case status, respond promptly to any USCIS requests, and manage stress through activities like yoga and community engagement. After approval, you will attend a biometrics appointment and then receive your conditional green card, valid for two years. Following receipt, update personal records, apply for a Social Security card, and prepare to file Form I-751 to remove conditions on residency before expiration. Throughout, maintaining organized documentation, staying in close contact with your attorney, and integrating into your community are essential for a smooth process and successful outcome.
summarization_1: Your marriage-based green card application involves several key steps starting with filing Form I-130 by early July 2023, followed by consular processing including the National Visa Center review, medical examination, and an interview at the U.S. embassy. After approval, you proceed to file Form I-485 to adjust status, attend a biometrics appointment, and prepare for a USCIS interview. The entire process typically takes about 8 to 12 months, with careful document preparation and timely responses to any requests being essential to meet your April 10, 2024 deadline.
